narrative.headline: 2020 Ram 2500: fuel and engine issues lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 158 complaints with NHTSA. The fuel and propulsion system is the most reported area at 25%, followed by the engine (20%) and two closely related fuel-system categories (19% each). No deaths are reported. A handful of reports mention a crash, fire, or injury, so the engine and fuel systems are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: The single biggest complaint area: 40 reports (25%). No crashes, fires, or injuries are reported here, but it is the most common thing owners flag.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 31 reports (20%), including 1 fire and 1 crash. Worth checking carefully given the fire report.

issues.title: Fuel System
issues.blurb: 30 reports (19%), linked to 1 crash and 1 injury. Several mention a fuel-system recall (Campaign 21V880000).

issues.title: Diesel
issues.blurb: 30 reports (19%), with 1 crash and 1 injury. Closely tied to the fuel-system complaints, often referencing the same diesel recall.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 20 reports (13%), tied to 1 fire and 1 crash. A smaller but recurring complaint area, sometimes appearing early in ownership.
